style=\"height:0.3552em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>⟩</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1.0361em;vertical-align:-0.2861em\"></span><span>⟨</span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.03588em\">g</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3361em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:-0.0359em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.02778em\">θ</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>(</span><span><span>k</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>)</span><span>,</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">v</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:-0.016em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.2861em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>⟩</span><span style=\"margin-right:1em\"></span><span><span>for all </span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span><span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8889em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span><span><span></span></span></span><span></span></span></span></span></span><span>=</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.10764em\">f</span><span>(</span><span>i</span><span>)</span><span>.</span></span></span></span></span></div>\n<blockquote>\n<p>We use the dot product as our similarity measure because it matches the softmax decoding used in language models; this is also the same setup used in <a href=\"https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.06538\" target=\"_blank\">prior work</a>.</p>\n</blockquote>\n<p>We quantify our construction's parameter efficiency via its <strong>fact-storage cost</strong>: we define this as the minimum number of parameters a model family needs to represent <em>every</em> possible fact map over <span><span><span><math>(K,V)(\\mathbf{K}, \\mathbf{V})</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>(</span><span>K</span><span>,</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span>. (Lower is better!)</p>\n<p>A simple counting argument gives us a lower-bound on fact-storage cost, assuming constant bits per parameter: any model family must use at least</p>\n<div><span><span><span><math>Ω ⁣(∣K∣log⁡∣V∣)\\Omega\\!\\big(|\\mathbf{K}| \\log |\\mathbf{V}|\\big)</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1.2em;vertical-align:-0.35em\"></span><span>Ω</span><span style=\"margin-right:-0.1667em\"></span><span><span>(</span></span><span>∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>lo<span style=\"margin-right:0.01389em\">g</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>∣</span><span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span></span></div>\n<p>parameters. Intuitively, there are <span><span><span><math>∣V∣∣K∣|\\mathbf{V}|^{|\\mathbf{K}|}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1.138em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span><span>∣</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.888em\"><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span>∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span> possible mappings from keys to values, so just identifying which mapping you want already takes <span><span><span><math>Θ(∣K∣log⁡∣V∣)\\Theta(|\\mathbf{K}| \\log |\\mathbf{V}|)</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>Θ</span><span>(</span><span>∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>lo<span style=\"margin-right:0.01389em\">g</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>∣</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span> bits.</p>\n<p>In this blog, we'll focus on using gated MLPs to store facts. Particularly, we'll consider MLPs of the form</p>\n<div><span><span><span><math>g(x)=D(σ(Gx)⊙(Ax)),g(\\mathbf{x}) = \\mathbf{D}\\big(\\sigma(\\mathbf{G}\\mathbf{x}) \\odot (\\mathbf{A}\\mathbf{x})\\big),</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.03588em\">g</span><span>(</span><span>x</span><span>)</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1.2em;vertical-align:-0.35em\"></span><span>D</span><span><span>(</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.03588em\">σ</span><span>(</span><span>Gx</span><span>)</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span><span>⊙</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1.2em;vertical-align:-0.35em\"></span><span>(</span><span>Ax</span><span>)</span><span><span>)</span></span><span>,</span></span></span></span></span></div>\n<p>where <span><span><span><math>σ\\sigma</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.4306em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.03588em\">σ</span></span></span></span></span> is any activation function (we focus on ReGLU or SwiGLU MLPs in this blog). Check out the paper for more details on non-gated MLPs!</p>\n<h3>Our goals</h3>\n<p>With the basic setup in place, we can now spell out what we want from our constructed MLPs. Compared to prior constructions, our approach is designed to achieve three goals:</p>\n<ol>\n<li>\n<p><strong>Handle general embeddings.</strong><br>\nPrior work focuses on key and value embeddings that are uniformly spherically distributed, but embeddings from language models are anisotropic and structured. We'll provide a construction that works for all but a measure-zero set of <strong>feasible</strong> embeddings (those with <span><span><span><math>ρ(V)>0\\rho(\\mathbf{V}) > 0</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>ρ</span><span>(</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>)</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.6444em\"></span><span>0</span></span></span></span></span>, as described below).</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p><strong>Match our information-theoretic baseline.</strong><br>\nIdeally, our constructed MLPs should match the baseline of <span><span><span><math>Θ(∣K∣log⁡∣V∣)\\Theta(|\\mathbf{K}| \\log |\\mathbf{V}|)</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>Θ</span><span>(</span><span>∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>lo<span style=\"margin-right:0.01389em\">g</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>∣</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span> parameters. We'll show that our construction matches this baseline up to a geometry-dependent constant:</p>\n<div><span><span><span><math>Θ ⁣([ρ(V)]−2 ∣K∣log⁡∣V∣),\\Theta\\!\\big([\\rho(\\mathbf{V})]^{-2}\\,|\\mathbf{K}| \\log |\\mathbf{V}|\\big),</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1.2141em;vertical-align:-0.35em\"></span><span>Θ</span><span style=\"margin-right:-0.1667em\"></span><span><span>(</span></span><span>[</span><span>ρ</span><span>(</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>)</span><span><span>]</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8641em\"><span style=\"top:-3.113em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span>−</span><span>2</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>lo<span style=\"margin-right:0.01389em\">g</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>∣</span><span><span>)</span></span><span>,</span></span></span></span></span></div>\n<p>where <span><span><span><math>ρ(V)\\rho(\\mathbf{V})</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>ρ</span><span>(</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span> is a decodability factor we introduce shortly. For \"well-spread\" embeddings such as spherical ones, <span><span><span><math>ρ(V)=Ω(1)\\rho(\\mathbf{V}) = \\Omega(1)</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>ρ</span><span>(</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>)</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>Ω</span><span>(</span><span>1</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span>, giving a scaling of <span><span><span><math>Θ ⁣(∣K∣log⁡∣V∣)\\Theta\\!\\big(|\\mathbf{K}| \\log |\\mathbf{V}|\\big)</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1.2em;vertical-align:-0.35em\"></span><span>Θ</span><span style=\"margin-right:-0.1667em\"></span><span><span>(</span></span><span>∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>lo<span style=\"margin-right:0.01389em\">g</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>∣</span><span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span></span>. This scaling asymptotically matches the parameter count of our information-theoretic baseline and requires a factor of <span><span><span><math>log⁡11∣V∣\\log^{11} |\\mathbf{V}|</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1.1484em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span><span>lo<span style=\"margin-right:0.01389em\">g</span></span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8984em\"><span style=\"top:-3.1473em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span>11</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>∣</span></span></span></span></span> fewer parameters than the lowest bound from prior work.</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p><strong>Be usable inside Transformers.</strong><br>\nWe want Transformers to be able to use our constructed MLPs for factual recall, with fact-storage cost that scales similarly to the MLP's fact-storage cost. Our construction handles this too, but we'll leave the details for <a href=\"https://hazyresearch.stanford.edu/blog/2025-11-28-mlps-p1\" target=\"_blank\">Part 1</a> of our blog post.</p>\n</li>\n</ol>\n<p>Here, we'll focus on the first two goals.</p>\n<hr>\n<h3>Roadmap</h3>\n<div style=\"text-align:center\"><img style=\"width:100%\" src=\"https://hazyresearch.stanford.edu/static/posts/2025-11-28-mlps/112625_construction_framework.png\" alt=\"Encoder-decoder structure\"><p><em>We factor the MLP into a gated encoder and a linear decoder to achieve asymptotically optimal scaling.</em></p></div>\n<p>We’re now ready to walk through the main ideas behind the construction. We’ll build up to the full MLP in a few steps:</p>\n<ol>\n<li>\n<p><strong>Encoder gadget.</strong><br>\nWe start with a simplified setting and construct a gated MLP that maps <span><span><span><math>∣K∣|\\mathbf{K}|</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span></span></span></span></span> inputs in <span><span><span><math>Rd\\mathbb{R}^d</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"></span><span><span>R</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>d</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span> to arbitrary scalar outputs using only <span><span><span><math>O(∣K∣)O(|\\mathbf{K}|)</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.02778em\">O</span><span>(</span><span>∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span> parameters. This “encoder gadget” is essentially optimal (up to a constant factor) for the scalar-output case. However, naïvely stacking <span><span><span><math>dd</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6944em\"></span><span>d</span></span></span></span></span> copies of this gadget to produce <span><span><span><math>dd</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6944em\"></span><span>d</span></span></span></span></span>-dimensional outputs still requires <span><span><span><math>Θ(d ∣K∣)\\Theta(d\\,|\\mathbf{K}|)</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>Θ</span><span>(</span><span>d</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span> parameters, which is too large to achieve our target scaling.</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p><strong>Decoding and <span><span><span><math>ρ(V)\\rho(\\mathbf{V})</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>ρ</span><span>(</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span>.</strong><br>\nDot-product decoding gives us an extra degree of freedom: our MLP doesn’t need to output the value embeddings themselves, only vectors that rank the correct value highest under dot-product similarity. This leads us to the <strong>decodability</strong> <span><span><span><math>ρ(V)\\rho(\\mathbf{V})</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>ρ</span><span>(</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span>, which measures how geometrically \"separated\" the value embeddings are. Small <span><span><span><math>ρ\\rho</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.625em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span>ρ</span></span></span></span></span> means the values are tightly packed and require more parameters to separate; large <span><span><span><math>ρ\\rho</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.625em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span>ρ</span></span></span></span></span> means they’re easier to distinguish. To take advantage of this, we introduce <strong>margin-optimal outputs</strong>, a set of output vectors that can maximally distinguish each value embedding from all other value embeddings.</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p><strong>Compression via a random projection decoder.</strong><br>\nThe final idea to achieve our target scaling is to <strong>compress</strong> the margin-optimal outputs to a lower-dimensional code while preserving the dot-product decoding constraints. We show that a simple random linear projection suffices via a Johnson–Lindenstrauss (JL) argument. With this choice of decoder, projecting into</p>\n<div><span><span><span><math>m=Θ ⁣([ρ(V)]−2log⁡∣V∣)m = \\Theta\\!\\big([\\rho(\\mathbf{V})]^{-2} \\log |\\mathbf{V}|\\big)</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.4306em\"></span><span>m</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1.2141em;vertical-align:-0.35em\"></span><span>Θ</span><span style=\"margin-right:-0.1667em\"></span><span><span>(</span></span><span>[</span><span>ρ</span><span>(</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>)</span><span><span>]</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8641em\"><span style=\"top:-3.113em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span>−</span><span>2</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>lo<span style=\"margin-right:0.01389em\">g</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>∣</span><span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span></span></div>\n<p>dimensions achieves perfect decoding accuracy with high probability.</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p><strong>Full encoder-decoder MLP.</strong><br>\nThe exponential reduction in output dimension from compression now lets us directly apply our stacked encoder gadget and, for many embeddings, allows us to achieve fact-storage cost asymptotically matching our information-theoretic baseline!\nThis yields our final construction:</p>\n<div><span><span><span><math>g(x)=D E(σ(Gx)⊙(Ax)),g(\\mathbf{x}) = \\mathbf{D}\\,\\mathbf{E}\\big(\\sigma(\\mathbf{G}\\mathbf{x}) \\odot (\\mathbf{A}\\mathbf{x})\\big),</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.03588em\">g</span><span>(</span><span>x</span><span>)</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1.2em;vertical-align:-0.35em\"></span><span>D</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>E</span><span><span>(</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.03588em\">σ</span><span>(</span><span>Gx</span><span>)</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span><span>⊙</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1.2em;vertical-align:-0.35em\"></span><span>(</span><span>Ax</span><span>)</span><span><span>)</span></span><span>,</span></span></span></span></span></div>\n<p>where <span><span><span><math>D\\mathbf{D}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6861em\"></span><span>D</span></span></span></span></span> is the JL-style decoder and <span><span><span><math>E(σ(Gx)⊙(Ax))\\mathbf{E}\\big(\\sigma(\\mathbf{G}\\mathbf{x}) \\odot (\\mathbf{A}\\mathbf{x})\\big)</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1.2em;vertical-align:-0.35em\"></span><span>E</span><span><span>(</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.03588em\">σ</span><span>(</span><span>Gx</span><span>)</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span><span>⊙</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1.2em;vertical-align:-0.35em\"></span><span>(</span><span>Ax</span><span>)</span><span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span></span> is the encoder. We show our construction can store any fact set over generic keys and decodable values with fact-storage cost</p>\n<div><span><span><span><math>Θ ⁣([ρ(V)]−2 ∣K∣log⁡∣V∣).\\Theta\\!\\big([\\rho(\\mathbf{V})]^{-2}\\,|\\mathbf{K}| \\log |\\mathbf{V}|\\big).</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1.2141em;vertical-align:-0.35em\"></span><span>Θ</span><span style=\"margin-right:-0.1667em\"></span><span><span>(</span></span><span>[</span><span>ρ</span><span>(</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>)</span><span><span>]</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8641em\"><span style=\"top:-3.113em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span>−</span><span>2</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>lo<span style=\"margin-right:0.01389em\">g</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>∣</span><span><span>)</span></span><span>.</span></span></span></span></span></div>\n</li>\n</ol>\n<hr>\n<h2>Our encoder gadget</h2>\n<p>We first focus on a simplified setting:</p>\n<blockquote>\n<p><em>Can we construct a gated MLP that maps a finite set of inputs in <span><span><span><math>Rd\\mathbb{R}^d</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"></span><span><span>R</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>d</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span> to arbitrary real numbers, using only <span><span><span><math>O(∣K∣)O(|\\mathbf{K}|)</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.02778em\">O</span><span>(</span><span>∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span> parameters for <span><span><span><math>∣K∣|\\mathbf{K}|</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span></span></span></span></span> datapoints?</em></p>\n</blockquote>\n<p>Our plan is to stack multiple copies of this encoder gadget to produce full outputs.</p>\n<p>We’ll start with a particularly nice choice of key embeddings as a warmup: <strong>two-hot difference vectors</strong> of standard basis vectors.</p>\n<h3>A toy setting: two-hot differences</h3>\n<p>Fix a dimension <span><span><span><math>dd</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6944em\"></span><span>d</span></span></span></span></span> and consider the set of inputs</p>\n<div><span><span><span><math>K2hot  =  { ei−ej  ∣  i≠j∈[d]},\\mathbf{K}_{\\text{2hot}} \\;=\\; \\big\\{\\, \\mathbf{e}_i - \\mathbf{e}_j \\;\\big|\\; i \\neq j \\in [d] \\big\\},</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8361em;vertical-align:-0.15em\"></span><span><span>K</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3361em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span><span>2hot</span></span></span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1.2em;vertical-align:-0.35em\"></span><span><span>{</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span><span>e</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span><span>−</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1.212em;vertical-align:-0.35em\"></span><span><span>e</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span preserveAspectRatio=\"xMinYMin\"><path d=\"M145 0 H188 V16 H145z M145 0 H188 V16 H145z\"></path></svg></span></span><span style=\"top:-2.862em\"><span style=\"height:2.606em\"></span><span><span>∣</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.35em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.854em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>∈</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span <span><span><span><math>∣K2hot∣=d2−d|\\mathbf{K}_{\\text{2hot}}| = d^2 - d</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>∣</span><span><span>K</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3361em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span><span>2hot</span></span></span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.8974em;vertical-align:-0.0833em\"></span><span><span>d</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8141em\"><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>2</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span><span>−</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.6944em\"></span><span>d</span></span></span></span></span> elements. For each ordered pair <span><span><span><math>(i,j)(i,j)</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>(</span><span>i</span><span>,</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span> with <span><span><span><math>i≠ji \\neq j</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8889em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span>i</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span><span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8889em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span><span><span></span></span></span><span></span></span></span></span></span><span>=</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.854em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span></span></span></span></span>, we choose an arbitrary target scalar <span><span><span><math>h(i,j)∈Rh(i,j) \\in \\mathbb{R}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>h</span><span>(</span><span>i</span><span>,</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span><span>)</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>∈</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.6889em\"></span><span>R</span></span></span></span></span>.</p>\n<p>Our goal is to build an MLP <span><span><span><math>enc:Rd→R\\mathrm{enc} : \\mathbb{R}^d \\to \\mathbb{R}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.4306em\"></span><span><span>enc</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>:</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"></span><span><span>R</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>d</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>→</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.6889em\"></span><span>R</span></span></span></span></span> such that</p>\n<div><span><span><span><math>enc(ei−ej)  =  h(i,j)for all i≠j.\\mathrm{enc}(\\mathbf{e}_i - \\mathbf{e}_j) \\;=\\; h(i,j) \\quad\\text{for all } i \\neq j.</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span><span>enc</span></span><span>(</span><span><span>e</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span><span>−</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1.0361em;vertical-align:-0.2861em\"></span><span><span>e</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.2861em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>)</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>h</span><span>(</span><span>i</span><span>,</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span><span>)</span><span style=\"margin-right:1em\"></span><span><span>for all </span></span><span>i</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span><span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8889em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span><span><span></span></span></span><span></span></span></span></span></span><span>=</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.854em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span><span>.</span></span></span></span></span></div>\n<p><strong>The encoder gadget construction</strong></p>\n<p>In this setting, we propose a simple weight construction for a 1-hidden-layer gated MLP, which we call our \"encoder gadget\":</p>\n<div><span><span><span><math>enc(x)  =  1d⊤ ⁣(ReLU(Idx)⊙(Ax)),\\mathrm{enc}(\\mathbf{x}) \\;=\\; \\mathbf{1}_d^\\top\\!\\big( \\mathrm{ReLU}(\\mathbf{I}_d \\mathbf{x}) \\odot (\\mathbf{A}\\mathbf{x}) \\big),</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span><span>enc</span></span><span>(</span><span>x</span><span>)</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1.2491em;vertical-align:-0.35em\"></span><span><span>1</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8991em\"><span style=\"top:-2.453em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>d</span></span></span><span style=\"top:-3.113em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>⊤</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.247em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:-0.1667em\"></span><span><span>(</span></span><span><span>ReLU</span></span><span>(</span><span><span>I</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3361em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>d</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>x</span><span>)</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span><span>⊙</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1.2em;vertical-align:-0.35em\"></span><span>(</span><span>Ax</span><span>)</span><span><span>)</span></span><span>,</span></span></span></span></span></div>\n<p>where</p>\n<div><span><span><span><math>A[p,q]  =  {0,p=q,−h(p,q),p≠q.\\mathbf{A}[p,q] \\;=\\; \\begin{cases} 0, &#x26; p = q, \\\\ -h(p,q), &#x26; p \\neq q. \\end{cases}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>A</span><span>[</span><span>p</span><span>,</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.03588em\">q</span><span>]</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:3em;vertical-align:-1.25em\"></span><span><span style=\"top:0em\"><span>{</span></span><span><span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:1.69em\"><span style=\"top:-3.69em\"><span style=\"height:3.008em\"></span><span><span>0</span><span>,</span></span></span><span style=\"top:-2.25em\"><span style=\"height:3.008em\"></span><span><span>−</span><span>h</span><span>(</span><span>p</span><span>,</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.03588em\">q</span><span>)</span><span>,</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1.19em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"width:1em\"></span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:1.69em\"><span style=\"top:-3.69em\"><span style=\"height:3.008em\"></span><span><span>p</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.03588em\">q</span><span>,</span></span></span><span style=\"top:-2.25em\"><span style=\"height:3.008em\"></span><span><span>p</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span><span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8889em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span><span><span></span></span></span><span></span></span></span></span></span><span>=</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.03588em\">q</span><span>.</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1.19em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span></span></span></span></span></span></span></div>\n<p>Intuitively, constructing the encoder gadget has two steps: 1) pick a gating term to cause different hidden neurons to select different \"portions\" of the inputs, then 2) set the <span><span><span><math>AA</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6833em\"></span><span>A</span></span></span></span></span> matrix so it fits the data.</p>\n<details><summary><strong>How does this work?</strong></summary><p>Plugging in a two-hot input <span><span><span><math>x=ei−ej\\mathbf{x} = \\mathbf{e}_i - \\mathbf{e}_j</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.4444em\"></span><span>x</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.7333em;vertical-align:-0.15em\"></span><span><span>e</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span><span>−</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.7305em;vertical-align:-0.2861em\"></span><span><span>e</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.2861em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span> with <span><span><span><math>i≠ji \\neq j</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8889em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span>i</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span><span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8889em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span><span><span></span></span></span><span></span></span></span></span></span><span>=</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.854em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span></span></span></span></span>:</p><ol>\n<li>\n<p><strong>Gating step.</strong></p>\n<div><span><span><span><math>ReLU(Id(ei−ej))=ReLU(ei−ej)=ei,\\mathrm{ReLU}\\big(\\mathbf{I}_d (\\mathbf{e}_i - \\mathbf{e}_j)\\big) = \\mathrm{ReLU}(\\mathbf{e}_i - \\mathbf{e}_j) = \\mathbf{e}_i,</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1.2em;vertical-align:-0.35em\"></span><span><span>ReLU</span></span><span><span>(</span></span><span><span>I</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3361em\"><span style=\"height:1.2em;vertical-align:-0.35em\"></span><span><span>e</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.2861em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>)</span><span><span>)</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span><span>ReLU</span></span><span>(</span><span><span>e</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.2861em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>)</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.6389em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span><span>e</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>,</span></span></span></span></span></div>\n<p>since <span><span><span><math>ei−ej\\mathbf{e}_i - \\mathbf{e}_j</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.7333em;vertical-align:-0.15em\"></span><span><span>e</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span><span>−</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.7305em;vertical-align:-0.2861em\"></span><span><span>e</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.2861em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span> has a <span><span><span><math>+1+1</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.7278em;vertical-align:-0.0833em\"></span><span>+</span><span>1</span></span></span></span></span> in coordinate <span><span><span><math>ii</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6595em\"></span><span>i</span></span></span></span></span> and a <span><span><span><math>−1-1</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.7278em;vertical-align:-0.0833em\"></span><span>−</span><span>1</span></span></span></span></span> in coordinate <span><span><span><math>jj</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.854em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span></span></span></span></span> (which gets zeroed out).</p>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p><strong>Linear step.</strong></p>\n<div><span><span><span><math>A(ei−ej)=Aei−Aej.\\mathbf{A}(\\mathbf{e}_i - \\mathbf{e}_j) = \\mathbf{A}\\mathbf{e}_i - \\mathbf{A}\\mathbf{e}_j.</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>A</span><span>(</span><span><span>e</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span><span>−</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1.0361em;vertical-align:-0.2861em\"></span><span><span>e</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.2861em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>)</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.8361em;vertical-align:-0.15em\"></span><span>A</span><span><span>e</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span><span>−</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.9722em;vertical-align:-0.2861em\"></span><span>A</span><span><span>e</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.2861em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>.</span></span></span></span></span></div>\n<p>The <span><span><span><math>ii</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6595em\"></span><span>i</span></span></span></span></span>-th coordinate of this vector is</p>\n<div><span><span><span><math>A[i,i]−A[i,j]=0−(−h(i,j))=h(i,j).\\mathbf{A}[i,i] - \\mathbf{A}[i,j] = 0 - (-h(i,j)) = h(i,j).</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>A</span><span>[</span><span>i</span><span>,</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>i</span><span>]</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span><span>−</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>A</span><span>[</span><span>i</span><span>,</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span><span>]</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.7278em;vertical-align:-0.0833em\"></span><span>0</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span><span>−</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>(</span><span>−</span><span>h</span><span>(</span><span>i</span><span>,</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span><span>))</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>h</span><span>(</span><span>i</span><span>,</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span><span>)</span><span>.</span></span></span></span></span></div>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p><strong>Elementwise product + sum.</strong><br>\nGating zeroes out all but the <span><span><span><math>ii</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6595em\"></span><span>i</span></span></span></span></span>-th coordinate:</p>\n<div><span><span><span><math>ReLU(Id(ei−ej))⊙(A(ei−ej))=ei⊙(A(ei−ej))=h(i,j) ei.\\mathrm{ReLU}(\\mathbf{I}_d (\\mathbf{e}_i - \\mathbf{e}_j)) \\odot (\\mathbf{A}(\\mathbf{e}_i - \\mathbf{e}_j)) = \\mathbf{e}_i \\odot (\\mathbf{A}(\\mathbf{e}_i - \\mathbf{e}_j)) = h(i,j)\\,\\mathbf{e}_i.</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span><span>ReLU</span></span><span>(</span><span><span>I</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3361em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>d</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span
