style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span><span>−</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1.0361em;vertical-align:-0.2861em\"></span><span><span>e</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.2861em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>))</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>h</span><span>(</span><span>i</span><span>,</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span><span>)</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span><span>e</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>.</span></span></span></span></span></div>\n<p>Summing with <span><span><span><math>1d⊤\\mathbf{1}_d^\\top</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1.1322em;vertical-align:-0.2831em\"></span><span><span>1</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"><span style=\"top:-2.4169em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>d</span></span></span><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>⊤</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.2831em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span> gives the scalar <span><span><span><math>h(i,j)h(i,j)</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>h</span><span>(</span><span>i</span><span>,</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span>.</p>\n</li>\n</ol></details>\n<p><strong>From toy to general embeddings</strong></p>\n<p>The two-step core idea behind the encoder gadget extends beyond the two-hot embeddings setting:</p>\n<ul>\n<li>Instead of using the identity as the gating matrix, we choose a more general gating matrix <span><span><span><math>G∈Rh×d\\mathbf{G} \\in \\mathbb{R}^{h \\times d}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.7252em;vertical-align:-0.0391em\"></span><span>G</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>∈</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"></span><span><span>R</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span>h</span><span>×</span><span>d</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span>. For analytic activation functions like Swish/SwiGLU and GeLU/GeGLU, our construction works for <strong>generic</strong> <span><span><span><math>G\\mathbf{G}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6861em\"></span><span>G</span></span></span></span></span> (for all but a measure 0 set of possible <span><span><span><math>G\\mathbf{G}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6861em\"></span><span>G</span></span></span></span></span>), so we can just sample a random matrix with i.i.d. Gaussian entries.</li>\n<li>Once the gating matrix is fixed, the network becomes <em>linear</em> in the remaining weights. This means we can solve a linear system to find an <span><span><span><math>A\\mathbf{A}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6861em\"></span><span>A</span></span></span></span></span> matrix that matches any desired outputs.</li>\n</ul>\n<details><summary><strong>The encoder gadget recipe for general embeddings:</strong></summary><p>Fix a generic <span><span><span><math>G\\mathbf{G}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6861em\"></span><span>G</span></span></span></span></span>. For each key <span><span><span><math>ki\\mathbf{k}_i</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8444em;vertical-align:-0.15em\"></span><span><span>k</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span>, the encoder gadget produces</p><div><span><span><span><math>ci  =  1h[σ(Gki)⊙(Aki)]=∑r=1hσ(Gki)r⟨ki, Ar⟩,c_i \\;=\\; \\mathbf{1}_h\\left[\\mathbf\\sigma(\\mathbf{G}\\mathbf{k}_i) \\odot (\\mathbf{A}\\mathbf{k}_i)\\right] = \\sum_{r=1}^h \\mathbf\\sigma(\\mathbf{G}\\mathbf{k}_i)_r \\langle \\mathbf{k}_i,\\, \\mathbf{A}_r\\rangle,</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.5806em;vertical-align:-0.15em\"></span><span><span>c</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span><span>1</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3361em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>h</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span><span style=\"top:0em\">[</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.03588em\">σ</span><span>(</span><span>G</span><span><span>k</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>)</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span><span>⊙</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span><span>(</span><span>A</span><span><span>k</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>)</span><span style=\"top:0em\">]</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:3.1032em;vertical-align:-1.2671em\"></span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:1.8361em\"><span style=\"top:-1.8829em;margin-left:0em\"><span style=\"height:3.05em\"></span><span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.02778em\">r</span><span>=</span><span>1</span></span></span></span><span style=\"top:-3.05em\"><span style=\"height:3.05em\"></span><span><span>∑</span></span></span><span style=\"top:-4.3em;margin-left:0em\"><span style=\"height:3.05em\"></span><span><span>h</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1.2671em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.03588em\">σ</span><span>(</span><span>G</span><span><span>k</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span><span>)</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.1514em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.02778em\">r</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>⟨</span><span><span>k</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>,</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span><span>A</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.1514em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.02778em\">r</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>⟩</span><span>,</span></span></span></span></span></div><p>so each encoder output is a linear function of <span><span><span><math>A\\mathbf{A}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6861em\"></span><span>A</span></span></span></span></span>.</p><p>Stacking these equations for all <span><span><span><math>ii</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6595em\"></span><span>i</span></span></span></span></span> and all <span><span><span><math>rr</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.4306em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.02778em\">r</span></span></span></span></span> gives a single linear system of the form</p><div><span><span><span><math>M v=c,\\mathbf{M}\\,\\mathbf{v} = \\mathbf{c},</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6861em\"></span><span>M</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">v</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.6389em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span>c</span><span>,</span></span></span></span></span></div><p>where:</p><ul>\n<li><span><span><span><math>v∈Rdh\\mathbf{v} \\in \\mathbb{R}^{dh}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.5782em;vertical-align:-0.0391em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">v</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>∈</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"></span><span><span>R</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span>d</span><span>h</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span> is just all the entries of <span><span><span><math>A\\mathbf{A}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6861em\"></span><span>A</span></span></span></span></span> flattened,</li>\n<li><span><span><span><math>c∈R∣K∣\\mathbf{c} \\in \\mathbb{R}^{|\\mathbf{K}|}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.5782em;vertical-align:-0.0391em\"></span><span>c</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>∈</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.888em\"></span><span><span>R</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.888em\"><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span>∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span> contains the desired outputs <span><span><span><math>(c1,…,c∣K∣)(c_1,\\dots,c_{|\\mathbf{K}|})</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1.1052em;vertical-align:-0.3552em\"></span><span>(</span><span><span>c</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3011em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>1</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>,</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>…</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>,</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span><span>c</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3448em\"><span style=\"top:-2.5198em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span>∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span></span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.3552em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span>, and</li>\n<li><span><span><span><math>M∈R∣K∣×(dh)\\mathbf{M} \\in \\mathbb{R}^{|\\mathbf{K}|\\times (dh)}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.7252em;vertical-align:-0.0391em\"></span><span>M</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>∈</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.888em\"></span><span><span>R</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.888em\"><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span>∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span><span>×</span><span>(</span><span>d</span><span>h</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span> depends only on the keys and the gating terms <span><span><span><math>σ(Gki)\\sigma(\\mathbf{G}\\mathbf{k}_i)</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.03588em\">σ</span><span>(</span><span>G</span><span><span>k</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span>.</li>\n</ul><p>In the paper, we show that the construction holds for generic <span><span><span><math>G\\mathbf{G}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6861em\"></span><span>G</span></span></span></span></span> with enough rows (roughly <span><span><span><math>hd≳∣K∣hd \\gtrsim |\\mathbf{K}|</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.9592em;vertical-align:-0.2296em\"></span><span>h</span><span>d</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>≳</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span></span></span></span></span>), for commonly-used activation functions like GeLU and Swish (and more generally any non-polynomial analytic function), for all but a measure-zero set of key embeddings, and for all choices of target scalars. For example, with Swish gate activations, our construction produces SwiGLU MLPs, like those from modern frontier models (e.g., <a href=\"https://www.llama.com/models/llama-3/\" target=\"_blank\">Llama</a> or <a href=\"https://qwenlm.github.io/blog/qwen3/\" target=\"_blank\">Qwen</a>).</p><blockquote>\n<p><strong>Encoder Construction (sketch)</strong></p>\n<ol>\n<li>Choose a generic gating matrix <span><span><span><math>G∈Rh×d\\mathbf{G} \\in \\mathbb{R}^{h \\times d}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.7252em;vertical-align:-0.0391em\"></span><span>G</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>∈</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"></span><span><span>R</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span>h</span><span>×</span><span>d</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span>.</li>\n<li>Compute the gating vectors <span><span><span><math>σ(Gki)\\sigma(\\mathbf{G}\\mathbf{k}_i)</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.03588em\">σ</span><span>(</span><span>G</span><span><span>k</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span> for all keys.</li>\n<li>Form and solve the resulting linear system to find <span><span><span><math>A\\mathbf{A}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6861em\"></span><span>A</span></span></span></span></span> such that <span><span><span><math>enc(ki)=ci\\mathrm{enc}(\\mathbf{k}_i) = c_i</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span><span>enc</span></span><span>(</span><span><span>k</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>)</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.5806em;vertical-align:-0.15em\"></span><span><span>c</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span> for every <span><span><span><math>ii</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6595em\"></span><span>i</span></span></span></span></span>.</li>\n</ol>\n</blockquote></details>\n<p><strong>Parameter count.</strong><br>\nA simple degrees-of-freedom argument shows that we need at least <span><span><span><math>∣K∣|\\mathbf{K}|</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span></span></span></span></span> parameters to specify arbitrary outputs on <span><span><span><math>∣K∣|\\mathbf{K}|</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span></span></span></span></span> inputs. When <span><span><span><math>dh=∣K∣dh=|\\mathbf{K}|</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6944em\"></span><span>d</span><span>h</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span></span></span></span></span>, our encoder uses</p>\n<div><span><span><span><math>2∣K∣+O(d+h)2|\\mathbf{K}| + O(d + h)</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>2∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span><span>+</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.02778em\">O</span><span>(</span><span>d</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span><span>+</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>h</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span></div>\n<p>parameters: one set of <span><span><span><math>∣K∣|\\mathbf{K}|</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span></span></span></span></span> parameters for <span><span><span><math>G∈Rh×d\\mathbf{G}\\in\\mathbb{R}^{h\\times d}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.7252em;vertical-align:-0.0391em\"></span><span>G</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>∈</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"></span><span><span>R</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span>h</span><span>×</span><span>d</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span>, one for <span><span><span><math>A∈Rh×d\\mathbf{A}\\in\\mathbb{R}^{h\\times d}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.7252em;vertical-align:-0.0391em\"></span><span>A</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>∈</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"></span><span><span>R</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span>h</span><span>×</span><span>d</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span>, and minor overhead for the readout. For <span><span><span><math>d,h=o(∣K∣)d, h = o(|\\mathbf{K}|)</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8889em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span>d</span><span>,</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>h</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>o</span><span>(</span><span>∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span>, this stays within a constant factor of the <span><span><span><math>∣K∣|\\mathbf{K}|</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span></span></span></span></span> lower bound.</p>\n<hr>\n<h3>Our first construction attempt (and why it’s not enough)</h3>\n<p>Our encoder gadget already gives us a simple way to solve the full problem.<br>\nIf the gadget lets us map from <span><span><span><math>Rd→R\\mathbb{R}^d \\to \\mathbb{R}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"></span><span><span>R</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>d</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>→</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.6889em\"></span><span>R</span></span></span></span></span>, then to map from <span><span><span><math>Rd→Rd\\mathbb{R}^d \\to \\mathbb{R}^d</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"></span><span><span>R</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>d</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>→</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"></span><span><span>R</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>d</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span> we can just <strong>stack <span><span><span><math>dd</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6944em\"></span><span>d</span></span></span></span></span> copies of the gadget — one per output coordinate!</strong></p>\n<p>Excitingly, this naïve construction is provably correct, and already works for a broader range of inputs/outputs than prior constructions! However, it uses <span><span><span><math>Θ(d ∣K∣)\\Theta(d\\,|\\mathbf{K}|)</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>Θ</span><span>(</span><span>d</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span> parameters, which can be much larger than our information-theoretic<br>\n<span><span><span><math>Ω(∣K∣log⁡∣V∣)\\Omega(|\\mathbf{K}| \\log |\\mathbf{V}|)</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>Ω</span><span>(</span><span>∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>lo<span style=\"margin-right:0.01389em\">g</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>∣</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span> bound when <span><span><span><math>d≫log⁡∣V∣d \\gg \\log |\\mathbf{V}|</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.7335em;vertical-align:-0.0391em\"></span><span>d</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>≫</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>lo<span style=\"margin-right:0.01389em\">g</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>∣</span></span></span></span></span> (e.g., for typical LLM embedding dimensions).</p>\n<p>To do better, we need to take advantage of our decoding mechanism.</p>\n<hr>\n<h2>Exploiting dot-product decoding</h2>\n<p>Let’s step back and look at the dot-product decoding condition again. For a fact set <span><span><span><math>ff</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8889em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.10764em\">f</span></span></span></span></span>, we require</p>\n<div><span><span><span><math>⟨vf(i), gθ(ki)⟩  >  max⁡j≠f(i)⟨vj, gθ(ki)⟩for all i.\\langle \\mathbf{v}_{f(i)},\\, g_\\theta(\\mathbf{k}_i) \\rangle \\;>\\; \\max_{j \\neq f(i)} \\langle \\mathbf{v}_j,\\, g_\\theta(\\mathbf{k}_i) \\rangle \\quad \\text{for all } i.</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1.1052em;vertical-align:-0.3552em\"></span><span>⟨</span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">v</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3448em\"><span style=\"top:-2.5198em;margin-left:-0.016em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.10764em\">f</span><span>(</span><span>i</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.3552em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>,</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.03588em\">g</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3361em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:-0.0359em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.02778em\">θ</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>(</span><span><span>k</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>)⟩</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1.716em;vertical-align:-0.966em\"></span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.4306em\"><span style=\"top:-2.309em;margin-left:0em\"><span style=\"height:3em\"></span><span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span><span><span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8889em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span><span><span></span></span></span><span></span></span></span></span></span><span>=</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.10764em\">f</span><span>(</span><span>i</span><span>)</span></span></span></span><span style=\"top:-3em\"><span style=\"height:3em\"></span><span><span>max</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.966em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span><span>⟨</span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">v</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:-0.016em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.2861em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>,</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.03588em\">g</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3361em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:-0.0359em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.02778em\">θ</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>(</span><span><span>k</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>)⟩</span><span style=\"margin-right:1em\"></span><span><span>for all </span></span><span>i</span><span>.</span></span></span></span></span></div>\n<p>A simple observation:</p>\n<blockquote>\n<p>We don’t need <span><span><span><math>gθ(ki)g_\\theta(\\mathbf{k}_i)</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.03588em\">g</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3361em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:-0.0359em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.02778em\">θ</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>(</span><span><span>k</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span> to equal <span><span><span><math>vf(i)\\mathbf{v}_{f(i)}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.7996em;vertical-align:-0.3552em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">v</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3448em\"><span style=\"top:-2.5198em;margin-left:-0.016em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.10764em\">f</span><span>(</span><span>i</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.3552em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span>! We only need it to rank the correct value with the largest dot-product score.</p>\n</blockquote>\n<p>This leads to two key ideas:</p>\n<ol>\n<li>We should pick MLP outputs that maximize the decoding margin — <em>and they don't necessarily need to equal <span><span><span><math>V\\mathbf{V}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6861em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span></span></span></span></span></em>.</li>\n<li>We can <strong>compress</strong> these output embeddings into a lower-dimensional space, <em>as long as the inequalities hold after decompression</em>.</li>\n</ol>\n<h3>Decodability <span><span><span><math>ρ(V)\\rho(\\mathbf{V})</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>ρ</span><span>(</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span></h3>\n<p>To motivate the first idea, we ask a simple question: <strong>how hard is it to tell the value embeddings apart using dot-product decoding?</strong> Some sets of value vectors are easy to distinguish; others are tightly clustered and require much larger margins to separate. We capture this with a single geometric quantity, the <em>decodability</em> <span><span><span><math>ρ(V)\\rho(\\mathbf{V})</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>ρ</span><span>(</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span>.</p>\n<p>Intuitively, <span><span><span><math>ρ(V)\\rho(\\mathbf{V})</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>ρ</span><span>(</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span> measures the <em>best achievable margin</em> between each value <span><span><span><math>vi\\mathbf{v}_i</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.5944em;vertical-align:-0.15em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">v</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:-0.016em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span> and all competing values under dot-product decoding. Larger <span><span><span><math>ρ\\rho</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.625em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span>ρ</span></span></span></span></span> means the values are well-separated and easy to decode; smaller <span><span><span><math>ρ\\rho</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.625em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span>ρ</span></span></span></span></span> means they’re packed together and require more parameters to separate.</p>\n<p>Before we define <span><span><span><math>ρ(V)\\rho(\\mathbf{V})</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>ρ</span><span>(</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span>, we note that it turns out to be a fundamental quantity for fact-storing MLPs. For <span><span><span><math>ρ(V)≤0\\rho(\\mathbf{V}) \\le 0</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>ρ</span><span>(</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>)</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>≤</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.6444em\"></span><span>0</span></span></span></span></span>, <strong>there are no MLPs</strong> that can store certain fact sets over <span><span><span><math>V\\mathbf{V}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6861em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span></span></span></span></span>. Additionally, <span><span><span><math>ρ(V)\\rho(\\mathbf{V})</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>ρ</span><span>(</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span> is predictive of fact-storage cost (equivalently, MLP size) across gradient-descent-trained and constructed MLPs (both ours and from prior work (NTK)!).</p>\n<div style=\"text-align:center\"><img style=\"width:100%\" src=\"https://hazyresearch.stanford.edu/static/posts/2025-11-28-mlps/112725_mlps_rho.png\" alt=\"Decodability of output embeddings\"><p><em><strong>Fact-storage cost depends on the decodability <span><span><span><math>ρ\\rho</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.625em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span>ρ</span></span></span></span></span> of the output embeddings.</strong></em></p></div>\n<p>Now, let's define <span><span><span><math>ρ(V)\\rho(\\mathbf{V})</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>ρ</span><span>(</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span>. Formally,</p>\n<div><span><span><span><math>ρ(V)=max⁡u1,…,u∣V∣ min⁡i≠j⟨vi−vj, ui⟩∥ui∥2 ∥vi−vj∥2.\\rho(\\mathbf{V}) = \\max_{\\mathbf{u}_1,\\dots,\\mathbf{u}_{|\\mathbf{V}|}} \\ \\min_{i \\neq j} \\frac{\\langle \\mathbf{v}_i - \\mathbf{v}_j,\\ \\mathbf{u}_i \\rangle} {\\lVert \\mathbf{u}_i \\rVert_2 \\,\\lVert \\mathbf{v}_i - \\mathbf{v}_j \\rVert_2}.</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>ρ</span><span>(</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>)</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:2.3991em;vertical-align:-0.9721em\"></span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.4306em\"><span style=\"top:-2.4em;margin-left:0em\"><span style=\"height:3em\"></span><span><span><span><span>u</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3173em\"><span style=\"top:-2.357em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.0714em\"><span style=\"height:2.5em\"></span><span><span>1</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.143em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>,</span><span>…</span><span>,</span><span><span>u</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3448em\"><span style=\"top:-2.3448em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.0714em\"><span style=\"height:2.5357em\"></span><span><span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>∣</span></span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.3695em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"top:-3em\"><span style=\"height:3em\"></span><span><span>max</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.9586em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span><span> </span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6679em\"><span style=\"top:-2.3479em;margin-left:0em\"><span style=\"height:3em\"></span><span><span><span>i</span><span><span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8889em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span><span><span></span></span></span><span></span></span></span></span></span><span>=</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span></span></span></span><span style=\"top:-3em\"><span style=\"height:3em\"></span><span><span>min</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.8882em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span><span></span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:1.427em\"><span style=\"top:-2.314em\"><span style=\"height:3em\"></span><span><span>∥</span><span><span>u</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span><span>∥</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3011em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>2</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>∥</span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">v</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:-0.016em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span><span>−</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">v</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:-0.016em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.2861em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span><span>∥</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3011em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>2</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"top:-3.23em\"><span style=\"height:3em\"></span><span style=\"border-bottom-width:0.04em\"></span></span><span style=\"top:-3.677em\"><span style=\"height:3em\"></span><span><span>⟨</span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">v</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:-0.016em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span><span>−</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">v</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:-0.016em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.2861em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>,</span><span> </span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span><span>u</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>⟩</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.9721em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span>.</span></span></span></span></span></div>\n<p>This is the largest worst-case (normalized) margin <span><span><span><math>⟨vi−vj, ui⟩\\langle \\mathbf{v}_i - \\mathbf{v}_j,\\ \\mathbf{u}_i \\rangle</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>⟨</span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">v</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:-0.016em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span><span>−</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1.0361em;vertical-align:-0.2861em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">v</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:-0.016em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span style=\"height:0.2861em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>,</span><span> </span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span><span>u</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>⟩</span></span></span></span></span> we can give every value by choosing appropriate output vectors <span><span><span><math>ui\\mathbf{u}_i</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.5944em;vertical-align:-0.15em\"></span><span><span>u</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span>.</p>\n<p>Given our definition of <span><span><span><math>ρ(V)\\rho(\\mathbf{V})</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>ρ</span><span>(</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span>, a natural choice of MLP output is to take, for each value, the <strong>margin-optimal output direction</strong></p>\n<div><span><span><span><math>ui⋆∈arg⁡max⁡u∈Sd−1min⁡j≠i⟨vi−vj, u⟩∥vi−vj∥2.\\mathbf{u}_i^\\star \\in \\arg\\max_{\\mathbf{u} \\in \\mathbb{S}^{d-1}} \\min_{j\\neq i} \\frac{\\langle \\mathbf{v}_i - \\mathbf{v}_j,\\, \\mathbf{u} \\rangle} {\\lVert \\mathbf{v}_i - \\mathbf{v}_j \\rVert_2}.</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.9474em;vertical-align:-0.2587em\"></span><span><span>u</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6887em\"><span style=\"top:-2.4413em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span <span><span><span><math>vi−vj\\mathbf{v}_i - \\mathbf{v}_j</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.7333em;vertical-align:-0.15em\"></span><span><span style=\"height:0.2861em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span> (for <span><span><span><math>j≠ij\\neq i</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8889em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span><span><span><span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.6595em\"></span><span>i</span></span></span></span></span>) — geometrically, it's the direction that most robustly separates <span><span><span><math>vi\\mathbf{v}_i</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.5944em;vertical-align:-0.15em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">v</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:-0.016em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span> from all other values. <a href=\"https://hazyresearch.stanford.edu/static/posts/2025-11-28-mlps/FactStoringMLPs.pdf\" target=\"_blank\">Our paper</a> contains a more detailed justification of <span><span><span><math>ui⋆\\mathbf{u}_i^\\star</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.9474em;vertical-align:-0.2587em\"></span><span><span>u</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6887em\"><span style=\"top:-2.4413em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>⋆</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.2587em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span>. Here we'll simply foreshadow that <span><span><span><math>ui⋆\\mathbf{u}_i^\\star</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.9474em;vertical-align:-0.2587em\"></span><span><span>u</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6887em\"><span style=\"top:-2.4413em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>⋆</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.2587em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span> turns out to be the optimal output for our decoder.</p>\n<h3>Compressing margin-optimal outputs</h3>\n<p>The second key idea is to use <em>compression</em> to reduce the dimension of the MLP outputs. Conveniently, dot-product decoding only depends on the <strong>sign</strong> of certain inner products:</p>\n<div><span><span><span><math>⟨vi−vj, ui⋆⟩>0for all j≠i.\\langle \\mathbf{v}_i - \\mathbf{v}_j,\\, \\mathbf{u}_i^\\star \\rangle > 0 \\quad\\text{for all } j \\neq i.</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>⟨</span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">v</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:-0.016em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span><span>−</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1.0361em;vertical-align:-0.2861em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">v</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:-0.016em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.2861em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>,</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span><span>u</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.7387em\"><span style=\"top:-2.453em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span><span style=\"top:-3.113em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>⋆</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.247em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>⟩</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.8889em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span>0</span><span style=\"margin-right:1em\"></span><span><span>for all </span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span><span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8889em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span><span><span></span></span></span><span></span></span></span></span></span><span>=</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.6595em\"></span><span>i</span><span>.</span></span></span></span></span></div>\n<p>If we can compress and decompress the <span><span><span><math>ui⋆\\mathbf{u}_i^\\star</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.9474em;vertical-align:-0.2587em\"></span><span><span>u</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6887em\"><span style=\"top:-2.4413em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>⋆</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.2587em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span> while preserving these signs, decoding will still work.</p>\n<p>Our decoder stems from the following observation: a Johnson–Lindenstrauss–style <strong>random linear projection</strong> <span><span><span><math>D∈Rd×m\\mathbf{D} \\in \\mathbb{R}^{d \\times m}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.7252em;vertical-align:-0.0391em\"></span><span>D</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>∈</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"></span><span><span>R</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span>d</span><span>×</span><span>m</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span> with</p>\n<div><span><span><span><math>m=Θ ⁣(ρ(V)−2log⁡∣V∣)m = \\Theta\\!\\big(\\rho(\\mathbf{V})^{-2} \\log |\\mathbf{V}|\\big)</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.4306em\"></span><span>m</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1.2141em;vertical-align:-0.35em\"></span><span>Θ</span><span style=\"margin-right:-0.1667em\"></span><span><span>(</span></span><span>ρ</span><span>(</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span><span>)</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8641em\"><span style=\"top:-3.113em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span>−</span><span>2</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>lo<span style=\"margin-right:0.01389em\">g</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>∣</span><span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span></span></div>\n<p>is enough to keep all the relevant inner-product signs intact with high probability.</p>\n<details><summary><strong>Why does this work?</strong></summary><p>After compression,</p><div><span><span><span><math>ui⋆↦D⊤ui⋆,vi−vj↦D⊤(vi−vj),\\mathbf{u}_i^\\star \\mapsto \\mathbf{D}^\\top\\mathbf{u}_i^\\star, \\qquad \\mathbf{v}_i - \\mathbf{v}_j \\mapsto \\mathbf{D}^\\top(\\mathbf{v}_i - \\mathbf{v}_j),</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span><span>−</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1.0361em;vertical-align:-0.2861em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">v</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:-0.016em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.05724em\">j</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.2861em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>,</span><span> </span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span><span>u</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.7387em\"><span style=\"top:-2.453em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span><span style=\"top:-3.113em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>⋆</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.247em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>⟩</span><span>.</span></span></span></span></span></div><p>Random projections preserve all pairwise inner products up to a small (less than <span><span><span><math>ρ(V)\\rho(\\mathbf{V})</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>ρ</span><span>(</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span>) distortion when</p><div><span><span><span><math>m=Ω(ρ(V)−2log⁡∣V∣).m = \\Omega(\\rho(\\mathbf{V})^{-2} \\log |\\mathbf{V}|).</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.4306em\"></span><span>m</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1.1141em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>Ω</span><span>(</span><span>ρ</span><span>(</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span><span>)</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8641em\"><span style=\"top:-3.113em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span>−</span><span>2</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>lo<span style=\"margin-right:0.01389em\">g</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>∣</span><span>)</span><span>.</span></span></span></span></span></div><p>Since the original inner products have a margin of at least <span><span><span><math>ρ(V)\\rho(\\mathbf{V})</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>ρ</span><span>(</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span>, distortions smaller than <span><span><span><math>ρ(V)\\rho(\\mathbf{V})</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>ρ</span><span>(</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span> cannot flip the signs. This means that decoding continues to work after compression!</p><blockquote>\n<p><strong>Decoder Construction (sketch)</strong></p>\n<ol>\n<li>Start with the margin-optimal outputs <span><span><span><math>U⋆∈R∣V∣×d\\mathbf{U}^\\star \\in \\mathbb{R}^{|\\mathbf{V}| \\times d}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.7278em;vertical-align:-0.0391em\"></span><span><span>U</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6887em\"><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>⋆</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>∈</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.888em\"></span><span><span>R</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.888em\"><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>∣</span><span>×</span><span>d</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span>.</li>\n<li>Sample a random projection matrix <span><span><span><math>D∈Rd×m\\mathbf{D} \\in \\mathbb{R}^{d \\times m}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.7252em;vertical-align:-0.0391em\"></span><span>D</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>∈</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"></span><span><span>R</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span>d</span><span>×</span><span>m</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span> (e.g., i.i.d. Gaussian).</li>\n<li>Form compressed codes\n<div><span><span><span><math>C=U⋆D.\\mathbf{C} = \\mathbf{U}^\\star \\mathbf{D}.</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6861em\"></span><span>C</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.7387em\"></span><span><span>U</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.7387em\"><span style=\"top:-3.113em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>⋆</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>D</span><span>.</span></span></span></span></span></div>\n</li>\n<li>Decode by projecting back up:\n<div><span><span><span><math>dec(c)=Dc.\\mathrm{dec}(\\mathbf{c}) = \\mathbf{D} \\mathbf{c}.</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span><span>dec</span></span><span>(</span><span>c</span><span>)</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.6861em\"></span><span>Dc</span><span>.</span></span></span></span></span></div>\nThen <span><span><span><math>dec(ci)≈ui⋆\\mathrm{dec}(\\mathbf{c}_i) \\approx \\mathbf{u}_i^\\star</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span><span>dec</span></span><span>(</span><span><span>c</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>)</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>≈</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.9474em;vertical-align:-0.2587em\"></span><span><span>u</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6887em\"><span style=\"top:-2.4413em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>⋆</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.2587em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span>, and the decoding inequalities are preserved.</li>\n</ol>\n</blockquote></details>\n<p>The key insight: now that we can reduce the output dimension from <span><span><span><math>dd</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6944em\"></span><span>d</span></span></span></span></span> to <span><span><span><math>Θ(ρ(V)−2log⁡∣V∣)\\Theta(\\rho(\\mathbf{V})^{-2}\\log|\\mathbf{V}|)</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1.0641em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>Θ</span><span>(</span><span>ρ</span><span>(</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span><span>)</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8141em\"><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span>−</span><span>2</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>lo<span style=\"margin-right:0.01389em\">g</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>∣</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span> using our decoder, we can revisit our stacked-encoder idea and finally make it parameter-efficient!</p>\n<hr>\n<h2>Putting everything together: the full construction</h2>\n<p>Let's combine the encoder and decoder into a single MLP that stores an arbitrary fact set over generic keys and decodable values. The resulting model has the form</p>\n<div><span><span><span><math>g(x)  =  D E(σ(Gx)⊙(Ax)),g(\\mathbf{x}) \\;=\\; \\mathbf{D}\\,\\mathbf{E}\\big(\\sigma(\\mathbf{G}\\mathbf{x}) \\odot (\\mathbf{A}\\mathbf{x})\\big),</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.03588em\">g</span><span>(</span><span>x</span><span>)</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1.2em;vertical-align:-0.35em\"></span><span>D</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>E</span><span><span>(</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.03588em\">σ</span><span>(</span><span>Gx</span><span>)</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span><span>⊙</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2222em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1.2em;vertical-align:-0.35em\"></span><span>(</span><span>Ax</span><span>)</span><span><span>)</span></span><span>,</span></span></span></span></span></div>\n<p>where <span><span><span><math>(G,A,E)(\\mathbf{G}, \\mathbf{A}, \\mathbf{E})</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>(</span><span>G</span><span>,</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>A</span><span>,</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>E</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span> form the <strong>encoder</strong>, <span><span><span><math>D\\mathbf{D}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6861em\"></span><span>D</span></span></span></span></span> is the <strong>decoder</strong>, and <span><span><span><math>σ\\sigma</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.4306em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.03588em\">σ</span></span></span></span></span> is a gating activation function (e.g., ReLU, GeLU, Swish). The encoder produces a compressed code in <span><span><span><math>Rm\\mathbb{R}^m</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6889em\"></span><span><span>R</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6644em\"><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>m</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span>; the decoder maps it back into <span><span><span><math>Rd\\mathbb{R}^d</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"></span><span><span>R</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8491em\"><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>d</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span> so that the output dot-product-decodes to the correct value embedding.</p>\n<details><summary><strong>To see our full recipe:</strong></summary><blockquote>\n<p><strong>Our MLP Construction (sketch)</strong></p>\n<ol>\n<li>\n<p><strong>Choose compressed outputs.</strong><br>\nCompute margin-optimal outputs <span><span><span><math>U⋆∈R∣V∣×d\\mathbf{U}^\\star\\in \\mathbb{R}^{|\\mathbf{V}| \\times d}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.7278em;vertical-align:-0.0391em\"></span><span><span>U</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6887em\"><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>⋆</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>∈</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.888em\"></span><span><span>R</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.888em\"><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>∣</span><span>×</span><span>d</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span> and draw a random projection</p>\n<div><span><span><span><math>D∈Rm×d,m=Θ ⁣(ρ(V)−2log⁡∣V∣).\\mathbf{D} \\in \\mathbb{R}^{m \\times d}, \\qquad m = \\Theta\\!\\big(\\rho(\\mathbf{V})^{-2} \\log |\\mathbf{V}|\\big).</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.7252em;vertical-align:-0.0391em\"></span><span>D</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>∈</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1.0935em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span><span>R</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8991em\"><span style=\"top:-3.113em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span>m</span><span>×</span><span>d</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>,</span><span style=\"margin-right:2em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>m</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1.2141em;vertical-align:-0.35em\"></span><span>Θ</span><span style=\"margin-right:-0.1667em\"></span><span><span>(</span></span><span>ρ</span><span>(</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span><span>)</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8641em\"><span style=\"top:-3.113em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span>−</span><span>2</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>lo<span style=\"margin-right:0.01389em\">g</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>∣</span><span><span>)</span></span><span>.</span></span></span></span></span></div>\n<p>Form compressed codes</p>\n<div><span><span><span><math>C=U⋆D⊤.\\mathbf{C} = \\mathbf{U}^\\star \\mathbf{D}^\\top.</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6861em\"></span><span>C</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.8991em\"></span><span><span>U</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.7387em\"><span style=\"top:-3.113em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>⋆</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span><span>D</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8991em\"><span style=\"top:-3.113em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>⊤</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>.</span></span></span></span></span></div>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p><strong>Construct the encoder.</strong><br>\nTreat <span><span><span><math>C\\mathbf{C}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6861em\"></span><span>C</span></span></span></span></span> as the target outputs and use the encoder to find weights<br>\n<span><span><span><math>(G,A,E)(\\mathbf{G}, \\mathbf{A}, \\mathbf{E})</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>(</span><span>G</span><span>,</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>A</span><span>,</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>E</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span> such that</p>\n<div><span><span><span><math>enc(ki)=cf(i)for all i.\\mathrm{enc}(\\mathbf{k}_i) = \\mathbf{c}_{f(i)} \\quad \\text{for all } i.</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span><span>enc</span></span><span>(</span><span><span>k</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3117em\"><span style=\"top:-2.55em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span>i</span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.15em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>)</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1.0496em;vertical-align:-0.3552em\"></span><span><span>c</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.3448em\"><span style=\"top:-2.5198em;margin-left:0em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span style=\"margin-right:0.10764em\">f</span><span>(</span><span>i</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.3552em\"><span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:1em\"></span><span><span>for all </span></span><span>i</span><span>.</span></span></span></span></span></div>\n</li>\n<li>\n<p><strong>Assemble the full model.</strong></p>\n<div><span><span><span><math>g(x)=D enc(x).g(\\mathbf{x}) = \\mathbf{D}\\,\\mathrm{enc}(\\mathbf{x}).</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.03588em\">g</span><span>(</span><span>x</span><span>)</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>D</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span><span>enc</span></span><span>(</span><span>x</span><span>)</span><span>.</span></span></span></span></span></div>\n</li>\n</ol>\n</blockquote></details>\n<p>Our main theoretical result shows that our proposed construction satisfies our goals of asymptotic scaling and generality:</p>\n<blockquote>\n<p><strong>Theorem (Full construction, informal).</strong><br>\nFor any fact set <span><span><span><math>ff</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8889em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.10764em\">f</span></span></span></span></span>, generic keys <span><span><span><math>K\\mathbf{K}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6861em\"></span><span>K</span></span></span></span></span>, and values <span><span><span><math>V\\mathbf{V}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6861em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span></span></span></span></span> with <span><span><span><math>ρ(V)>0\\rho(\\mathbf{V}) > 0</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>ρ</span><span>(</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>)</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:0.6444em\"></span><span>0</span></span></span></span></span>, the above construction yields a fact MLP <span><span><span><math>gg</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.625em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.03588em\">g</span></span></span></span></span> that stores <span><span><span><math>ff</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8889em;vertical-align:-0.1944em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.10764em\">f</span></span></span></span></span> and has fact-storage cost</p>\n<div><span><span><span><math>Θ ⁣([ρ(V)]−2 ∣K∣log⁡∣V∣).\\Theta\\!\\big([\\rho(\\mathbf{V})]^{-2}\\, |\\mathbf{K}| \\log |\\mathbf{V}|\\big).</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1.2141em;vertical-align:-0.35em\"></span><span>Θ</span><span style=\"margin-right:-0.1667em\"></span><span><span>(</span></span><span>[</span><span>ρ</span><span>(</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>)</span><span><span>]</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8641em\"><span style=\"top:-3.113em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span>−</span><span>2</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>lo<span style=\"margin-right:0.01389em\">g</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>∣</span><span><span>)</span></span><span>.</span></span></span></span></span></div>\n</blockquote>\n<p>For value embeddings with <span><span><span><math>ρ(V)=Ω(1)\\rho(\\mathbf{V}) = \\Omega(1)</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>ρ</span><span>(</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>)</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span><span>=</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.2778em\"></span></span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>Ω</span><span>(</span><span>1</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span>, such as uniformly-spherical embeddings, this matches our information-theoretic baseline of <span><span><span><math>Ω(∣K∣log⁡∣V∣)\\Omega(|\\mathbf{K}| \\log |\\mathbf{V}|)</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span>Ω</span><span>(</span><span>∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>lo<span style=\"margin-right:0.01389em\">g</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>∣</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span>, improving over bounds from prior work by a factor of <span><span><span><math>log⁡11∣V∣\\log^{11} |\\mathbf{V}|</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1.1484em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span><span>lo<span style=\"margin-right:0.01389em\">g</span></span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8984em\"><span style=\"top:-3.1473em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"height:2.7em\"></span><span><span><span>11</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>∣</span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>∣</span></span></span></span></span>.</p>\n<p>Since we have matched the parameter count asymptotics of our information-theoretic baseline, we can next ask how many bits per parameter our construction uses. For the two-hot example, we actually achieve a constant number of bits per parameter (leading to information-theoretically optimal scaling in the total number of MLP bits), and, for embeddings such as the uniform spherical embeddings assumed by <a href=\"https://arxiv.org/abs/2412.06538\" target=\"_blank\">prior constructions</a>, our construction uses <span><span><span><math>O(log⁡∣K∣)O(\\log |\\mathbf{K}|)</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:1em;vertical-align:-0.25em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.02778em\">O</span><span>(</span><span>lo<span style=\"margin-right:0.01389em\">g</span></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.1667em\"></span><span>∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span><span>)</span></span></span></span></span> bits per parameter. In both cases, our construction comes within a single log factor of the information-theoretically optimal rate, when measured in bits.</p>\n<p>Table 1 summarizes how our construction compares to bounds on prior fact-storing MLP constructions.</p>\n<table><thead><tr><th>Construction</th><th align=\"right\">Parameters</th><th align=\"right\">Hidden Sizes</th><th>Assumptions on <span><span><span><math>K\\mathbf{K}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6861em\"></span><span>K</span></span></span></span></span></th><th>Assumptions on <span><span><span><math>V\\mathbf{V}</math></span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.6861em\"></span><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>)</span><span><span>]</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8141em\"><span style=\"top:-3.063em;margin-right:0.05em\"><span style=\"margin-right:0.01597em\">V</span><span>)</span><span><span>]</span><span><span><span><span style=\"height:0.8913em\"><span style=\"top:-2.931em;margin-right:0.0714em\"><span style=\"height:2.5em\"></span><span><span><span>−</span><span>2</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span>∣</span><span>K</span><span>∣</span></span></span></span></span><span></span></span><span><span
